From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Estonian athletics competitor

**Maile Mangusson** (born 3 July 1973) is an [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") athletics competitor.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

She was born in [Vändra](/wiki/V%C3%A4ndra "Vändra") Selsoviet. In 1997 she graduated from [Tallinn Pedagogical Institute](/wiki/Tallinn_Pedagogical_Institute "Tallinn Pedagogical Institute")'s Faculty of Physical Education.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

She started her athletics exercising in 1987, coached by Avo Jussi. 1991-2005 her coach was Uno Källe. She is multiple-times Estonian champion in different sport disciplines. She has been a member of Estonian national athletics team.[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

Records:[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)

* 400 m: 56,57 (2001)
* 800 m: 2.04,23 (2001)
* 1000 m: 2.45,54 (2000)
* heptathlon: 5139 (1998)

In 1997 she was named as Best Women Athlete of [Pärnu County](/wiki/P%C3%A4rnu_County "Pärnu County").[[1]](#cite_note-ESBL-1)
